summary: Simplified the chat empty state by merging three separate lines into a single clear message. Removed the visually jarring background pill on the privacy notice that created unnecessary clutter and inconsistent hierarchy.

Improved the pre-meeting setup screen when the camera is turned off. Instead of showing a bare icon with no context, users now see a styled avatar circle with a clear "Camera is off" label, providing immediate visual feedback about their camera state.
Repo health: 62/100 — No test coverage and multiple unhandled promise rejections across critical user-facing paths create silent failures that are hard to diagnose in production.

Moved the chat message character counter from floating over the input text to sitting below it, preventing the counter from overlapping with long messages. Simplified the warning colors to a clearer two-state system that only appears when nearing the limit.

Improved the empty state shown in the background effects dialog when the camera is off. Previously it was a single line of plain text with no visual cue; now it displays a camera-off icon, a clear heading, and a helpful hint telling users to enable their camera to preview background effects.

The toggle now responds immediately when clicked, giving users proper visual feedback that their preference was saved.

When chat fails to connect, the input area is now hidden instead of showing a grayed-out, disabled text field with a hard-to-read error message. The existing error banner with its "Refresh Page" button already tells users what to do, so the redundant disabled input was just visual clutter.
